pi_items.source: Task description (step 5) and export_result.sh verification script match exactly
Confirmed in task description, env.json, seed_demodb.py, and all scripts
Task description step 1 specifies exact subclass names; verifier.py checks for these three classes
Task description step 2 explicitly requires adding 'Tier' property (String) to Hotels class; verifier checks for this
Task description step 4 specifies HasFreeWifi boolean for BudgetHotels; verifier checks COUNT of records where HasFreeWifi = true
Task description step 4 specifies HasPool boolean for MidRangeHotels; verifier checks COUNT of records where HasPool = true
Task description step 4 specifies HasSpa boolean for LuxuryHotels; verifier checks COUNT of records where HasSpa = true
seed_demodb.py defines exactly 15 hotels in seed_hotels() function; setup_task.sh modifies star ratings but does not delete any records
Setup modifies Hotel de Crillon to 2 stars (only hotel with Stars <= 2); verified in setup_task.sh and budget_query computation
Setup modifies The Savoy to 3 stars (only hotel with Stars = 3); verified in setup_task.sh and midrange_query computation
All remaining hotels after budget/midrange categories; includes Hotel Artemide (4), Adlon Kempinski (5), and 11 other 5-star hotels
seed_demodb.py hardcodes 15 hotel records with real hotel names and coordinates; no external data downloads

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetic DemoDB travel agency dataset with 15 real luxury hotel names (Hotel Adlon Kempinski Berlin, Park Hyatt Tokyo, The Savoy London, etc.) created programmatically via OrientDB SQL INSERT commands. The setup script modifies star ratings to create deterministic distribution: 1 budget hotel (de Crillon, 2 stars), 1 midrange hotel (The Savoy, 3 stars), and 13 luxury hotels (4+ stars). The task requires creating three inheritance subclasses (BudgetHotels, MidRangeHotels, LuxuryHotels) of Hotels with tier-specific properties (Tier on base class, HasFreeWifi/HasPool/HasSpa on subclasses). The verifier checks schema structure (25pts), data migration (30pts), property population (25pts), and report file generation (20pts) with a 60-point passing threshold.
